title: The Latte Factor: $5/Day into S&P 500 (2014)
description: What redirecting $5/day (the cost of a daily latte) into the S&P 500 every month from January 1, 2014 would be worth today. Total invested: ~$20,075 over 11 years. This is the 'latte factor' — the idea that small daily expenses, redirected to investing, compound into meaningful wealth.
opportunity_cost_note: An investor who kept $5/day as cash (in a checking account earning 0%) would have $20,075 today. The investing scenario produces ~$38,000 — an additional $17,925 from market returns.
educational_note: The 'latte factor' is a powerful mental model for visualizing small habit changes. Note: This scenario uses monthly DCA on the first trading day of each month. Real returns vary by exact purchase dates. The $5/day also does not account for the tax treatment of gains in a taxable account.
